conflicts.description: Emergency-fund transfer timing is inconsistent: chapter 1 frames a morning move-in-day transfer, chapter 2 cites an 11:43 PM prior-night transfer, and chapter 4 provides bank-record timestamps.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:176 (7:12 morning transfer framing); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:553 (11:43 PM prior-night transfer framing); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:979 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:981 (bank records at 06:12 transfer and 06:34 cash withdrawal on 08/28).
conflicts.canonical_value: Use chapter-4 documentary timestamps: 08/28 transfer at 06:12 and cash withdrawal at 06:34.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 4 includes explicit bank evidence used in legal/financial causality, so it is the hardest-to-change and most reliable version.

conflicts.description: Within chapter 17, weekly transfer terms change from $400 to $500 after additional disclosures.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5702 ($400 from each weekly check); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6014 (line revised to $500); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6208 (Friday transfer discussed as $500).
conflicts.canonical_value: $500 weekly transfer with Friday proof deadline.
conflicts.resolution_note: The later term is an explicit amendment triggered by new information and governs subsequent behavior.

conflicts.description: Chapter 17 first sets an eight-week basement arrangement with week-six review, then revises to a 30-day provisional arrangement with day-14 review.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5706 (8-week term, week-6 review); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6018 (30-day provisional, day-14 review); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6212 (day-14 review scheduling).
conflicts.canonical_value: 30-day provisional basement residence with day-14 review and immediate termination on breach.
conflicts.resolution_note: The revised clause is later in-sequence and tied to a major trust breach, so it supersedes the initial draft terms.

conflicts.description: The previous sheet tracked a higher legacy title-loan amount; manuscript chapter 13 updates the active post-claim balance to a specific deficiency figure.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4204 (net payout $4,980 applied to lienholder); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4246 (deficiency stated as $1,693.11); snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4504 (still owes 'sixteen ninety-three').
conflicts.canonical_value: RidgeLine active deficiency is $1,693.11 after insurer payout application.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 13 lender paperwork and speakerphone balance quote provide current, actionable debt state and override legacy sheet totals.

conflicts.description: Prior sheet estimated casino wages at a different weekly baseline; chapter 14 provides a concrete paystub.
conflicts.canonical_value: Casino baseline set to about $800 gross weekly (about $600 net on shown stub).
conflicts.resolution_note: Direct paystub evidence is more reliable than prior estimate ranges.
